statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: Archived audit trail only. Keep this out of the normal tweak surface.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: timeBeginPeriod function
links.summary: Microsoft documents the general timer-resolution tradeoff, noting that higher resolution can reduce overall system performance and power efficiency. The page confirms the feature area but does not publish the DisableLowQosTimerResolution registry key.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Timer Resolution
links.summary: Microsoft documents timer resolution as a system behavior that affects timing precision and system cost, but the page does not publish the DisableLowQosTimerResolution registry key.

links.title: nohuto mirror: low-QoS timer resolution gate
links.summary: The decompiled kernel routine checks KeDisableLowQosTimerResolution before toggling the process policy bit and calling ExpUpdateTimerResolution, and the mirror docs record the same DisableLowQosTimerResolution = 1 gate.

links.title: Repo system research notes for kernel registry values
links.summary: The repo documents DisableLowQosTimerResolution = 1 as personal research and ties it to KeDisableLowQosTimerResolution.

links.title: Current app implementation
links.summary: The provider writes DisableLowQosTimerResolution = 1 for the current tweak.
